lessons.title: Introduction
coreTruths: Catechism is a sounding from above, meaning it is the voice of God from Heaven.
It is the systematic teaching that teaches us what we should believe, how we should believe it, and how we should behave.

ageCallout.basics: Catechism is how we learn God's rules and what Christians believe, like a special message from Heaven.

lessons.title: Religion. Christianity
coreTruths: Religion is the relationship and communication of man with God.
Other religions begin from man and go towards God, but the Christian religion begins from God and descends to man.

ageCallout.basics: While some people try to build a bridge up to God, our faith is special because God built the bridge to us by becoming man.

lessons.title: Other Religions and Christianity
coreTruths: Christianity is a monotheistic religion, meaning Christians believe in one, personal God.
Christianity draws the truth from Divine Revelation, which includes both oral Holy Tradition and written Holy Scripture.

ageCallout.basics: We believe in one God. We learn His truth from the Bible and the Holy Tradition passed down in the Church.

lessons.title: God Exists
coreTruths: The existence of the world, with its multitude of precise natural laws, leads us to believe in the existence of God.
God planted the voice of our conscience within us to commend us when we do what is right and censure us when we step out of line.

ageCallout.basics: We know God exists because of the beautiful universe He created, and because He gave us a conscience to know right from wrong.

lessons.title: What God is
coreTruths: God is the highest, infinite, unlimited, perfect spiritual Being from Whom derived all that has been created.
He is present everywhere, beyond time, all-knowing, all-wise, and all-mighty.

ageCallout.basics: God does not have a physical body like us; He is a perfect Spirit. He knows everything, is everywhere, and rules everything with love.

lessons.title: The Holy Trinity
coreTruths: God is one, but trinitarian, including three persons: the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.
The mystery of the Holy Trinity cannot be comprehended with human reasoning; it is lived and understood through faith.

ageCallout.basics: The Holy Trinity is a great mystery. God is One, but three Persons: the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit, and we believe this through faith.

lessons.title: God the Father
coreTruths: God is our Father in a moral sense, a Father with infinite love for His creatures Who always receives sinners when they repent.
Christ is the Son of God by His nature and substance before all ages, whereas we are children of God by grace.

ageCallout.basics: We are God's children by His grace, but Jesus is God's Son by nature. God loves us and always forgives us when we repent.

lessons.title: The Son is God
coreTruths: The Son, Jesus Christ, existed from the Beginning, together with the Father, and is perfectly God.
While the Son became man, He did not cease to be God, remaining always in the bosom of the Father.

ageCallout.basics: Jesus is the Son of God and is perfectly God. Even when He became a man on earth, He never stopped being God.

lessons.title: The Holy Spirit is God
coreTruths: The Holy Spirit is the third Person of the Holy Trinity and is equal to the Father and the Son.
The Holy Spirit is uncreated God, worshipped and glorified together with the Father and the Son.

ageCallout.basics: The Holy Spirit is the third Person of the Holy Trinity. He is truly God and we worship Him just as we worship the Father and the Son.

lessons.title: Some Attributes of the Holy Trinity
coreTruths: The Holy Trinity is one in essence and inseparable, where the three Persons co-inhere in one another.
The Father is unborn, the Son is born of the Father, and the Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father.

ageCallout.basics: The Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are always united and fit perfectly inside one another. They are completely equal.

lessons.title: The Creation of the World
coreTruths: God created all of the visible and invisible world out of nothing by His word alone.
God created the world out of love to make it a participant in His glory, joy, and bliss.

ageCallout.basics: God didn't need to make the world, but He created everything out of nothing because He is perfect Love and wanted to share His joy.

lessons.title: The Creation of the Spiritual World
coreTruths: Angels are insubstantial, spiritual, immortal, and self-governed beings that serve God and protect mankind.
Lucifer abused his freedom, attempted to ascend above the stars to be like God, and became Satan.

ageCallout.basics: Angels serve God and protect us. One angel became proud and wanted to be like God, which is how he became Satan.

lessons.title: The Creation of Man
coreTruths: Man is the connecting link between the spiritual and material worlds, comprised of a spiritual soul and a material body.
God created man according to His image, meaning He gave man freedom and the power to choose.

ageCallout.basics: You are special because you connect the physical world with the spiritual world. God gave you free will so you can choose to do good.

lessons.title: The Origin of the Soul and the Condition of Primitive Man
coreTruths: The soul and the body are conceived and created at the same time with the cooperation of God and parents.
Man was placed in Paradise and given a command not to eat from the tree of knowledge as a form of fasting to strengthen his character.

ageCallout.basics: Your soul and body were created at the exact same time. God gave the first humans a rule in the garden to help strengthen their character.

lessons.title: Original Sin and its Consequences
coreTruths: Original sin is the denunciation of God, the attempt of man to disenthrone God and become God himself.
The consequences of original sin are spiritual death, bodily death, and the distortion of man's nature, making the mind dark and the will weak.

ageCallout.basics: Original sin wasn't just about eating a fruit; it was humans wanting to be God. This disobedience broke our nature and brought death into the world.

lessons.title: The Salvation of Fallen Man
coreTruths: God promised salvation to man because he sinned through the influence of Satan, giving man a second chance.
Only someone who was without sin, Jesus Christ, was able to save sinful humanity and return us to the house of God.

ageCallout.basics: Because humans were tricked by Satan, God gave us a second chance. Only Jesus, who has no sin, could save us and bring us back to God.

lessons.title: The Incarnation of the Logos
coreTruths: The second Person of the Holy Trinity was incarnated from the Holy Spirit and the Virgin Mary, becoming perfect man.
The infinite God condescended to humble Himself to put on human flesh so that man could become God-like.

ageCallout.basics: God loved us so much that He became a human being. He humbled Himself and was born in a manger so that we could be raised up to Heaven.

lessons.title: The God-Man
coreTruths: In Christ the God-man, two natures are united: the divine and the human, without confusion, distinct and separate.
The Son of God assumed human nature completely to restore it to Heaven through His Resurrection and Ascension.

ageCallout.basics: Jesus is a miracle: He is 100% God and 100% human. He took on our human nature to carry it back to Heaven.

lessons.title: The Divine and Human Nature of Christ
coreTruths: The Incarnate Word assumed human nature with all of its properties and is similar in all things to us, but without sin.
His human nature united with His divine nature becomes divinized, allowing us to become divine in the moral sense and be saved.

ageCallout.basics: Jesus experienced human things like hunger and fatigue, but He never sinned. By joining His divine nature to our human nature, He saves us.

lessons.title: The Three Offices of Christ
coreTruths: Christ has three indissolubly united offices: the Prophetic to teach truth, the High Priest to sanctify, and the Royal to redeem.
As Triumphant Victor, Christ used His Royal Authority to descend to Hades, destroy its gates, and resurrect the dead.

ageCallout.basics: Jesus fixes everything the Fall broke: He is a Prophet who teaches us truth, a High Priest who forgives our sins, and a King who defeated death.

lessons.title: The Holy Spirit
coreTruths: The Holy Spirit is true God, equal with the Son and the Father, and proceeds from the Father.
The Holy Spirit holds together the institution of the Church, works through the sacraments, and sanctifies the faithful.

ageCallout.basics: The Holy Spirit proceeds from the Father and is truly God. He guides the Church and gives us grace through the sacraments.

lessons.title: Redemption
coreTruths: Christ took upon Himself the sins of humanity, atoned for man, and reconciled him with God.
God respects the freedom of man and does not compel anyone; man must open his heart and cooperate to receive redemption.

ageCallout.basics: Jesus did the hard work of saving us on the Cross, but God doesn't force us. We have to choose to open our hearts and accept His salvation.

lessons.title: Divine Grace
coreTruths: Divine Grace is the good will of God offered freely to man as a gift, without his deserving it.
It is indispensable for salvation, guiding man to repentance, regeneration, and sanctification, though man must use free will to accept it.

ageCallout.basics: We can't earn God's Grace; it is a free gift. God knocks at the door of our hearts with this Grace, and we just have to open the door.

lessons.title: Church
coreTruths: The Church is the mystical body of Christ, and Christ is the head.
It is the ark of salvation, and it is One, Holy, Catholic, and Apostolic.

ageCallout.basics: The Church is the mystical body of Christ. Being part of the Church is like being on an ark that keeps us safe and brings us to God's Kingdom.

lessons.title: Sacraments
coreTruths: Sacraments are holy ceremonies through which divine grace is transferred to man to cleanse, sanctify, and guide him to salvation.
There are seven sacraments, four of which (Baptism, Chrismation, Eucharist, Repentance/Confession) are obligatory for salvation.

ageCallout.basics: The Church has seven sacraments. They are special holy ceremonies where God physically gives us His grace to heal and save us.

lessons.title: Baptism
coreTruths: Baptism is performed with three immersions in water and cleanses the person from original sin and all other sins.
The baptized person dies to sin, is reborn, and becomes a member of the mystical body of Christ.

ageCallout.basics: Baptism washes away all sin, including original sin. Going into the water means dying to bad things, and coming out means being reborn into God's family.

lessons.title: Chrismation
coreTruths: Chrismation is an apostolic practice performed immediately after baptism with Holy Chrism prepared at the Ecumenical Patriarchate.
It gives the baptized person the gifts and armour of the Holy Spirit to continue their spiritual battle.

ageCallout.basics: After Baptism, you received Holy Chrism. This acts like spiritual armour, giving you the gifts of the Holy Spirit to help you fight against sin.

lessons.title: Holy Eucharist
coreTruths: The Holy Eucharist is truly the body and blood of Christ, not a symbol.
Its main purpose is the forgiveness of sins, spiritual nourishment, and uniting man with God.

ageCallout.basics: Holy Communion isn't just a symbol; the bread and wine actually become the true Body and Blood of Christ. We need it to feed our souls just like food feeds our bodies.

lessons.title: Repentance and Confession
coreTruths: Christ gave only the Apostles and their successors the authority to forgive sins.
Repentance is a changing of the mind and a way of life, and confession is revealing sins to a Priest or Bishop to be cleansed.

ageCallout.basics: Repentance means wanting to change our bad habits. We confess our sins to a priest because Jesus gave priests the special authority to forgive sins for Him.

lessons.title: Holy Priesthood
coreTruths: The priesthood is God-sent and has three ranks: the diaconate, the presbyterate, and the episcopate.
Every priesthood must have canonical apostolic succession to be true and genuine.

ageCallout.basics: Jesus gave His authority to the Apostles, who passed it down to our Bishops and Priests today. This is called Apostolic Succession.

lessons.title: Matrimony
coreTruths: Marriage is established by God, reaffirmed by Christ, and is an indissoluble sacrament.
It is an icon of the mystical unity of the bridegroom, Christ, with the bride, the Church.

ageCallout.basics: God created marriage to be permanent. The love between a husband and wife is supposed to look like the unbreakable love Jesus has for the Church.

lessons.title: Holy Unction
coreTruths: Holy Unction is established by God for bodily healing and the forgiveness of sins.
The sacrament is celebrated with olive oil, scripture, and prayers, but does not replace Repentance and Confession.

ageCallout.basics: The Church uses holy oil and prayer to ask God for physical healing and forgiveness. It's a special comfort when we are sick.

lessons.title: Concerning Death
coreTruths: Death is not the end or a catastrophe; it is a stage, a starting point, and a birth into eternity.
Christ abolished the fear of death by becoming the first born from the dead.

ageCallout.basics: Because of Jesus, death is not an end. It is actually just a starting point and our birth into eternity.

lessons.title: What Happens after Death
coreTruths: Immediately after death, the separated soul undergoes a particular judgement.
At the final judgement during the Second Coming, body and soul will be reunited, and Christ will separate the just from the sinners.

ageCallout.basics: Right after we die, our soul has a particular judgement. Later, when Jesus returns, we will get our bodies back for the final judgement.

lessons.title: The Resurrection of the Dead
coreTruths: Before the final judgement, the dead will be resurrected and the soul will be reunited with the body.
The resurrected body will be spiritualized and made immortal.

ageCallout.basics: When Jesus comes back, the dead will be resurrected. God will reunite our souls with a new body that is immortal and imperishable.

lessons.title: Eternal Life and Eternal Hell
coreTruths: The righteous will enjoy eternal life and happiness, whereas sinners will live in eternal punishment and despair.
This state will be eternal, without any change or amelioration.

ageCallout.basics: Our choices matter. God prepared an eternal kingdom of happiness for those who love Him, and we must try our best to follow Him there.
